title: Replace vaultrs with raw HTTP for zeroization
description:
The vault backend currently depends on vaultrs, which deserializes secret values through serde_json::Map<String, serde_json::Value>. This means secret bytes pass through non-Zeroizing heap allocations before reaching SecretValue, defeating the end-to-end zeroization guarantee that the custom JSON parser in secretx-core was written to provide.

The fix is to make raw HTTP calls to the Vault KV v2 REST API directly and parse the response with the existing json_extract_string_field parser in secretx-core, which never copies secret bytes into non-Zeroizing memory.

This is a known design mismatch, not a vaultrs bug. vaultrs makes no zeroization promises and its API surface is incompatible with this constraint.

Severity depends on threat model. The exposure window is short (a few allocations), but it is real. Relevant for cold-boot or memory-forensics attack scenarios.
